name: Al-Arqam Islamic School
mission: TO MEET THE EDUCATIONAL NEEDS OF THE MUSLIM CHILDREN LIVING WITHIN THE GREATER SACRAMENTO AREA.
theoryOfChange: Al-Arqam Islamic School has published its 'Student Learning Goals' as part of its Islamic Education curriculum. These goals serve as a results framework, outlining the intended impact on students across five key areas: living Islam, becoming lifelong learners, effective communicators, citizens of the world, and honoring themselves physically and spiritually.
scoreSummary: Al-Arqam Islamic School shows exceptional impact and moderate alignment.

evaluationSources: Western Association of Schools and Colleges (WASC)
Accreditation (Western Association of Schools and Colleges (WASC))

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Good donor alignment with manageable risk. Evidence quality is solid but still evolving in places.

zakat.claim_evidence: The donation page for Al-Arqam Islamic School (Sacramento, CA) lists methods for donations (checks, PayPal, Zelle) but does not explicitly mention "zakat" or have a dedicated zakat fund or campaign. (Source: https://www.alarqamislamicschool.org/apps/pages/index.jsp?uREC_ID=1440031&type=d)

all_citations.claim: The school reported total revenue of $4,330,166 and offers an IB Diploma Programme.
The organization maintains a program expense ratio of 98.1%, working capital of 9.1 months, and a fundraising efficiency of $0.00 per $1 raised.
Al-Arqam Islamic School & College Preparatory utilizes an admissions process to serve the educational needs of the community.
The school manages student attendance and administrative policies to maintain educational standards.
The institution enforces a student honor code and parent policy handbook to ensure a structured learning environment.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization earns a 67/100 due to its excellent financial health and direct impact on community capacity building, though its alignment score is moderated by its specific local focus.
areas_for_improvement: Limited public documentation regarding specific long-term student outcomes and post-graduation impact metrics
Moderate alignment for specialized Muslim donor segments due to a focus on local capacity building over global urgent relief

dimension_explanations.alignment: While providing a vital service to the local Muslim community, the alignment score of 24/50 reflects a moderate donor fit for those seeking urgent global interventions, as the school focuses on long-term educational stability in Sacramento [1][5].
dimension_explanations.impact: The school demonstrates high impact in capacity building by dedicating 98.1% of its $4,330,166 revenue to its educational programs [1][2]. Its commitment to academic rigor is evidenced by its status as an IB Diploma Programme provider and its comprehensive student honor code [3][6].

baseline_narrative.headline: Al-Arqam Islamic School provides rigorous college preparatory and International Baccalaureate education to Muslim youth in the Greater Sacramento area.
strengths: Exceptional program expense ratio of 98.1%, ensuring nearly all funds directly support educational services
Strong financial stability with 9.1 months of working capital available
Highly efficient fundraising operations with a cost of $0.00 per $1 raised
baseline_narrative.summary: Al-Arqam Islamic School & College Preparatory is dedicated to meeting the educational needs of Muslim children through a curriculum that includes the International Baccalaureate (IB) Diploma Programme [1][3]. The institution operates with a high degree of financial efficiency, reporting total revenue of $4,330,166 and maintaining a focus on academic excellence and student conduct [2][6].

case_against.summary: The primary limitations are <cite id="1">geographic specificity</cite> and <cite id="1">transparency gaps</cite> regarding long-term student outcomes.

confidence.notes: Strong financial data but limited outcome transparency.

alignment.explanation: The school offers a <cite id="6">strong Islamic identity</cite> and serves the educational needs of Muslim children, though it is <cite id="1">SADAQAH-ELIGIBLE only</cite> and does not process zakat.

credibility.explanation: Donors can trust Al-Arqam due to its <cite id="1">100% independent board</cite> and its long-standing <cite id="3">28-year track record</cite> in the Sacramento area. The school's <cite id="4">IB World School authorization</cite> provides external validation of its academic standards.
credibility.improvement: To strengthen credibility, the school should <cite id="1">pursue a Candid Seal of Transparency</cite> and publish annual impact reports detailing student graduation and college placement rates.

impact.explanation: Impact is driven by a <cite id="1">98.1% program expense ratio</cite> and a <cite id="1">resilient 9.1 months of working capital</cite>. The school utilizes a <cite id="3">capacity-building delivery model</cite> that invests in long-term community human capital.
impact.improvement: Impact measurement would benefit from <cite id="1">publishing consistent beneficiary counts</cite> to allow for cost-effectiveness benchmarking against peer educational institutions.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who prioritize long-term investment in Islamic educational infrastructure and value high financial efficiency in local community institutions.
donor_motivations: Want to support the development of the next generation of Muslim leaders
Value the integration of Islamic studies with rigorous IB academics
Prefer organizations with extremely low overhead and zero fundraising costs
Focus on domestic community capacity building
giving_considerations: Impact is localized to the Greater Sacramento area
Financial data is strong, but detailed outcome reporting is limited
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking to fulfill zakat obligations or those looking for immediate international humanitarian relief.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade C reflects verified financial efficiency and academic accreditation, but lacks public longitudinal data on student outcomes.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: The school creates impact by providing a dual-curriculum of rigorous academics and Islamic values to produce college-ready, ethically grounded graduates.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that the educational model successfully prepares students for higher education and maintains Islamic values.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: As a growing institution with a 3.1% CAGR, Al-Arqam has capacity to expand its facilities and scholarship programs.

peer_comparison.differentiator: Distinguished by a <cite id="1">100.0% program ratio</cite>, which is significantly higher than the <cite id="1">peer median of 89.6%</cite>.

strengths.detail: The organization maintains a <cite id="1">98.1% program expense ratio</cite>, significantly exceeding the <cite id="1">peer program ratio median of 89.6%</cite> for similar educational non-profits.
Al-Arqam demonstrates strong financial health with <cite id="1">9.1 months of working capital</cite> and a <cite id="2">3-year revenue CAGR of 3.1%</cite>.
The school is an <cite id="3">authorized IB World School</cite> and provides a <cite id="4">documented college preparatory curriculum</cite> that includes specialized Islamic studies.

strengths_deep_dive: Al-Arqam's primary strength lies in its ability to convert nearly every dollar of revenue into direct educational impact. With <cite id="2">fundraising efficiency of $0.00 per $1 raised</cite>, the school avoids the high overhead costs typical of many non-profits. This efficiency is paired with a <cite id="1">100% independent board of directors</cite>, ensuring objective oversight of the school's <cite id="2">$2,633,655 in total assets</cite>. Furthermore, the school's longevity—operating since <cite id="3">1998</cite>—establishes it as a mature institution within the Sacramento Muslim community, capable of sustaining complex programs like the <cite id="6">International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme</cite>.
rich_narrative.summary: founded in 1998, Al-Arqam Islamic School & College Preparatory serves the Greater Sacramento area by providing a <cite id="1">rigorous college preparatory and International Baccalaureate (IB) education</cite> for Muslim youth. The institution operates with a high degree of financial efficiency, reporting <cite id="2">total revenue of $4,155,519</cite> in the 2023 tax year. A defining characteristic of the school's financial model is its <cite id="1">98.1% program expense ratio</cite>, indicating that nearly all functional expenses are directed toward educational services. The school's curriculum is comprehensive, featuring the <cite id="3">IB Diploma Programme, Islamic Studies, and Arabic language instruction</cite> alongside standard California academic requirements. As an <cite id="4">authorized IB World School</cite>, Al-Arqam emphasizes both academic excellence and character development, governed by a <cite id="6">Student Honor Code of Conduct</cite> that integrates Islamic values with civic responsibility. While the school is primarily funded through <cite id="2">program service revenue totaling $3,925,159</cite>, it maintains a strong liquidity position with <cite id="1">9.1 months of working capital</cite> held in working capital, ensuring long-term institutional stability.
